description: Top-level work can run on import and may prevent safe tree shaking.
body: Use when moving initialization, registering globals, or changing library entrypoints. Do not assume unused imports are removed if modules have top-level side effects. Inspect package sideEffects metadata and bundler config. Verify build output or runtime behavior for affected entry. Risk includes duplicate initialization and larger bundles. Use when JavaScript Runtime and Tooling work touches repository conventions, toolchain files, changed symbols, validation commands, and runtime output. Do not use this guidance to override a more specific pack, user instruction, or repository contract. Actions: use the fact to constrain the change and test plan; inspect the current files first, keep the change scoped to the owning boundary, and record what evidence changed the decision. Validation signals include package-local checks, focused tests, build output, smoke runs, and command logs. Risk boundary: escalate or stop when the task involves broad rewrites, generated-file drift, stale assumptions, and unvalidated behavior changes, missing owner evidence, or live external state.
